purpose: Turn the deterministic repair path from a Hub-side recommendation/analysis layer into an end-to-end deterministic repair runtime with machine-readable RepairProcedureExecutionPlan, Worker RepairProcedureRunner, verification, persistence, learning, UI/API support, and full E2E tests.

review_basis.observed_problem: The Hub has strong deterministic repair analysis models, signatures, playbooks, safety policies and outcome-memory target models, but the Worker currently does not authoritatively execute machine-readable repair procedures. If the Worker only receives unstructured context text, the flow remains LLM-guided recommendation, not deterministic execution.
review_basis.core_judgement: Current state is a deterministic diagnosis and recommendation layer. Target state is a deterministic repair execution system.

global_principles: Deterministic repair decisions must be structured data, not only prompt/context text.
Hub remains the control plane: signature matching, procedure selection, safety classification, approval and repair plan authorization are Hub-owned.
Worker executes a Hub-issued RepairProcedureExecutionPlan or RepairStepExecutionEnvelope only inside explicit capabilities and approvals.
Worker may have a native RepairProcedureRunner, but it must not select arbitrary procedures or expand authority.
LLM escalation is allowed only as bounded diagnosis/proposal support when policy permits it; it must not replace deterministic repair execution.
Every mutation step requires preconditions, approval status, verification, rollback hints and audit.
Every repair outcome must be persisted, queryable and usable for negative learning/ranking.
If input is malformed, confidence is low, approval is missing, verification is impossible or safety is unknown, fail closed or escalate explicitly.
non_goals: Do not make the Worker infer repair procedure from natural language context.
Do not allow LLM-generated commands to execute as deterministic repair without Hub-issued procedure steps.
Do not auto-approve review_first or high_risk repair procedures.
Do not persist repair learning only in memory variables.
Do not skip verification after mutation steps.
Do not expose dangerous repair config as casual UI text fields without validation.
Do not implement hidden or unbounded repair actions.

milestones.title: Persistence, outcome memory and negative learning

tasks.title: Define RepairProcedureExecutionPlan schema
tasks.problem: The repair model defines procedures and safety policy, but the actual executable repair plan passed to Worker must be a typed machine-readable contract, not a prompt paragraph.
implementation_notes: Use Pydantic/dataclass plus optional JSON schema consistent with project style.
Plan should be immutable once authorized for execution.
Keep procedure steps narrow: collect_evidence, command_probe, command_mutation, file_patch_propose, file_patch_apply, service_restart, verification_probe, stop, escalate.
acceptance_criteria: RepairProcedureExecutionPlan includes plan_id, goal_id, task_id, procedure_id, problem_class, signature_id, signature_confidence, safety_class, approval_requirement, environment_facts_hash, created_by='hub', policy_decision_ref, context_bundle_ref, steps, verification_plan, rollback_hints, max_runtime_seconds and version.

Each step includes step_id, step_type, title, action, preconditions, expected_inputs, expected_outputs, mutation_candidate, action_safety_class, requires_approval, required_capabilities, allowed_tools, timeout_seconds, verification_after_step and rollback_hint_refs.
Unknown step_type is rejected by schema validation.
Mutation steps without verification_after_step are rejected unless explicitly marked as no-op/dry-run.
Plan schema rejects empty procedure_id, empty steps and missing policy_decision_ref.
Positive and negative fixture tests exist.
test_requirements: Schema validation test for valid service_start_failure plan.
Negative tests for unknown step_type, missing approval requirement, mutation without verification.
anti_patterns: Do not pass repair instructions as unstructured context only.
Do not let Worker fill missing procedure fields by guessing.

tasks.title: Extend ExecutionEnvelope with repair_procedure payload
tasks.problem: Worker needs to receive RepairProcedureExecutionPlan as an authoritative payload inside ExecutionEnvelope, not hidden in prompt/context text.
implementation_notes: Add repair_procedure: RepairProcedureExecutionPlan | None.
Add execution_mode values such as deterministic_repair_plan, deterministic_repair_step, repair_dry_run.
Add validation ensuring repair modes require repair_procedure.
acceptance_criteria: ExecutionEnvelope supports repair_procedure payload for deterministic repair modes.
ExecutionEnvelope validation rejects deterministic_repair_plan mode without repair_procedure.
ExecutionEnvelope validation rejects repair_procedure.created_by != 'hub' unless test compatibility explicitly allows it.
CapabilityGrant must include repair_execute or repair_diagnose depending on mode.
Envelope trace metadata includes repair_plan_hash and procedure_id.
Existing non-repair ExecutionEnvelope tests continue to pass.
test_requirements: Negative test: repair mode without repair_procedure.
Negative test: repair_procedure with worker-created authority.
anti_patterns: Do not encode repair_procedure only in context_envelope text.
Do not bypass CapabilityGrant validation for repair mode.

tasks.title: Define RepairStepExecutionEnvelope for stepwise execution
tasks.problem: Full procedure execution and single-step execution need a narrow step contract so Hub can choose to orchestrate each step itself or let Worker run bounded procedure mode.
implementation_notes: Step envelope should reference parent plan hash and exact step id.
Use for Hub-driven mode: Hub sends one step at a time to Worker.
acceptance_criteria: RepairStepExecutionEnvelope includes parent_plan_id, parent_plan_hash, step_id, step, current_state, approval_ref, context_refs, capability_snapshot_ref, audit_correlation_id and expected_result_schema.
Step envelope rejects step not present in parent plan if parent plan is supplied.
Mutation step without valid approval_ref is rejected before tool invocation.
Step envelope can be converted into ToolInvocationEnvelope for supported step types.
Tests prove tampered parent_plan_hash is rejected.
anti_patterns: Do not let Worker execute an arbitrary step id not present in the authorized plan.
Do not let single-step execution bypass procedure safety rules.

tasks.title: Define RepairExecutionResult and RepairStepResult contracts
tasks.problem: Repair execution needs structured results that distinguish diagnosis, mutation, verification, skipped, denied, approval-required, failed and escalated states.
implementation_notes: Map to WorkerResult v2 later, but keep repair-specific detail.
acceptance_criteria: RepairStepResult includes step_id, status, reason_code, started_at, ended_at, tool_result_refs, artifacts, evidence, verification_result, side_effects, rollback_hint_used and warnings.

RepairExecutionResult includes plan_id, procedure_id, status, completed_steps, skipped_steps, failed_step_id, approval_required_step_id, final_verification, outcome_label, artifacts, trace_bundle_ref and persisted_outcome_ref.
Status enum includes success, partial_success, denied, needs_approval, failed, escalated, verification_failed, cancelled and timeout.
Mutation step success requires side_effects and verification_result fields.
Results are serializable to JSON without secrets.
test_requirements: Negative test: mutation success without verification_result rejected or downgraded.
anti_patterns: Do not collapse repair result into one free-text summary.
Do not claim success when final verification is missing.

tasks.title: Define deterministic repair capability vocabulary
tasks.problem: Repair needs explicit capability names so WorkerPreflightGate and policy can distinguish diagnosis, proposal, low-risk repair, approval-gated mutation and high-risk escalation.
implementation_notes: Use canonical capability names and aliases if needed.
Do not reuse generic coding capability for repair execution.
acceptance_criteria: Capability vocabulary includes repair.detect, repair.diagnose, repair.plan, repair.execute.inspect, repair.execute.low_risk, repair.execute.approval_gated, repair.verify, repair.rollback, repair.outcome.persist and repair.llm_escalate.
High-risk repair actions require explicit repair.execute.approval_gated or stricter capability.
review_first/high_risk actions cannot run with only repair.diagnose or generic coding capability.
Unknown repair capability is denied by default.
Tests cover capability matrix for safe, review_first and high_risk procedure classes.
anti_patterns: Do not allow generic shell_execute alone to mean repair procedure authorization.
Do not collapse diagnosis and mutation capabilities.

tasks.title: Build Hub-side RepairProcedureExecutionPlan generator
tasks.problem: The Hub currently performs deterministic analysis, but it must produce an authorized executable plan from matched signature, diagnosis playbook and repair procedure model.
implementation_notes: Create repair_execution_plan_service.py rather than growing deterministic_repair_path_service too much.
Input: normalized_evidence, environment_facts, matched_signature outcome, selected playbook/procedure, policy decision.
Output: RepairProcedureExecutionPlan.
acceptance_criteria: High-confidence signature can generate deterministic RepairProcedureExecutionPlan without LLM.
Low-confidence/no-match outcome generates escalation or diagnosis-only plan, not mutation plan.
Ambiguous outcome generates review_required or evidence-collection plan.
Generated plan includes step safety classes, approval requirements and verification plan.
Generated plan includes environment_facts_hash and evidence refs, not huge raw logs.
Plan generation validates output schema before returning.
test_requirements: Low-confidence test produces no mutation steps.
anti_patterns: Do not ask LLM to invent the initial repair plan when deterministic signature is high confidence.
Do not generate mutation steps without safety class.

tasks.title: Convert diagnosis playbooks into executable non-mutating evidence steps
tasks.problem: Diagnosis playbooks currently describe collect/evaluate/branch/classify flow. They need deterministic step expansion into Worker-executable evidence collection steps.
implementation_notes: Map evidence_sources to allowed probes/commands using allowlisted templates.
Evidence collection must be inspect_only by default.
acceptance_criteria: service_status source maps to inspect-only status probe step.
error_logs source maps to bounded log-read probe step with max lines/time window.
port_state source maps to inspect-only port/process probe step.
runtime_state/container_state source maps to bounded inspect-only probe step.
Unknown evidence_source produces plan generation error or escalation, not arbitrary command.
All generated diagnosis steps have mutation_candidate=false.
anti_patterns: Do not convert evidence_source strings directly into shell commands.
Do not mutate system state during diagnosis.

tasks.title: Create bounded repair procedure catalog with explicit command templates
tasks.problem: Repair procedures must not be arbitrary text. Each bounded repair should be a catalog entry with preconditions, command templates, safety class, verification and rollback hints.
implementation_notes: Start with inspect-only and very low-risk bounded examples.
Avoid destructive fixes initially.
Use parameterized command templates with strict field validation.
acceptance_criteria: Catalog has entries for port_conflict inspect, service_start_failure inspect, compose_failure inspect, path_issue inspect and package_install_failure inspect.
Catalog may include low-risk repair only where rollback/verification is clear.
Each catalog entry declares procedure_id, problem_class, supported_platforms, preconditions, steps, safety_class, required_approval, verification and rollback_hints.
Command templates cannot contain unbound user-controlled string interpolation.
Catalog lookup by problem_class and environment facts returns deterministic candidates with ranking reasons.
anti_patterns: Do not store repair commands as unchecked free-form strings.
Do not include destructive commands in initial catalog.

tasks.title: Add LLM escalation envelope for unknown/ambiguous repair cases
tasks.problem: LLM escalation is part of the model, but it must produce bounded diagnosis/proposal artifacts, not execute repairs or bypass deterministic safety.
implementation_notes: LLM escalation should receive bounded evidence and output RepairProposalArtifact or diagnosis suggestion only.
It must never directly create executable mutation plan without Hub validation.
acceptance_criteria: Escalation allowed only for allowed reasons from LLM_ESCALATION_POLICY_MODEL.
Escalation is forbidden for single_high_confidence_without_contradictions and deterministic_path_succeeded.
Escalation output is RepairProposalArtifact or ReviewRequiredArtifact, not direct Worker repair execution.
Hub validates any LLM-suggested procedure against catalog/safety before execution.
Tests prove LLM escalation does not run for high-confidence deterministic path.
test_requirements: LLM proposal validation test with fake LLM.
anti_patterns: Do not let LLM output raw commands that Worker executes as deterministic repair.
Do not send unbounded logs/secrets to LLM escalation.

tasks.title: Add robust input validation and error handling around repair analysis
tasks.problem: Repair analysis functions often assume well-formed inputs. Deterministic systems need explicit malformed-input behavior.
implementation_notes: Add safe wrappers or validation helpers for normalized_evidence, environment_facts, signature catalog and playbook payloads.
Return structured errors rather than raw exceptions across service boundary.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed normalized_evidence returns reason_code='invalid_evidence_payload' or raises documented domain error caught by caller.
Missing environment_facts does not crash; it reduces confidence or returns structured error depending on required fields.
Unknown problem_class returns structured error/escalation, not KeyError.
Invalid regex in custom signature catalog is caught and reported.
Empty playbook steps are rejected during catalog validation.
Tests cover malformed evidence, missing fields, unknown problem class and invalid regex.
test_requirements: No raw stack trace from normal service API tests.
anti_patterns: Do not let malformed input crash the whole repair flow.
Do not hide invalid input by returning low confidence without reason.

tasks.title: Create Worker RepairProcedureRunner skeleton
tasks.problem: Worker currently lacks native awareness of deterministic repair procedures. It needs a RepairProcedureRunner that executes structured procedure steps under policy, not LLM text.
implementation_notes: Runner should accept ExecutionEnvelope with repair_procedure.
Start with dry_run and inspect-only steps before mutation steps.
Runner must not select procedure itself.
acceptance_criteria: RepairProcedureRunner class exists with run_plan(envelope) and run_step(step_envelope) or equivalent methods.
Runner rejects envelope without repair_procedure in deterministic repair mode.
Runner validates plan hash and policy refs before execution.
Runner supports dry_run returning planned steps without executing tools.
Runner supports inspect-only evidence collection steps through registered tools.
Runner returns RepairExecutionResult/WorkerResult, not raw text.
anti_patterns: Do not make Runner parse repair instructions from LLM response.
Do not let Runner choose a different procedure_id than the Hub-issued one.

tasks.title: Implement RepairProcedureRunner state machine
tasks.problem: Repair execution must follow allowed states and transitions instead of ad hoc loops.
implementation_notes: Use REPAIR_STATE_MODEL transitions as source or copy into worker contract module.
Keep state transitions explicit and traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Runner starts in detected/diagnosing or ready state according to plan type.
Runner only permits transitions allowed by REPAIR_STATE_MODEL or worker-specific repair state model.
Invalid transition returns failed/escalated with reason_code='invalid_repair_state_transition'.
Terminal states are succeeded, failed or escalated/cancelled/timeout.
Each transition emits trace/audit event.
test_requirements: Terminal state no further step execution test.
anti_patterns: Do not allow arbitrary jump from diagnosing to succeeded without verification.
Do not continue executing after terminal failure.

tasks.title: Map repair steps to ToolInvocationEnvelope
tasks.problem: Repair steps must execute via WorkerToolRegistry/ToolInvocationEnvelope, otherwise procedure runner can bypass existing worker enforcement.
implementation_notes: Map inspect/probe steps to read-only tools.
Map mutation steps to high-risk tools with approval.
acceptance_criteria: Every executable repair step is converted into a ToolInvocationEnvelope before tool execution.
Unknown step_type maps to denied, not arbitrary shell.
Required capabilities and allowed_tools from the step are enforced by WorkerPreflightGate/ToolRegistry.
Tool result is captured as RepairStepResult evidence/artifact refs.
Tests prove denied tool does not execute.
anti_patterns: Do not call subprocess directly from RepairProcedureRunner.
Do not bypass ToolInvocationEnvelope because repair is 'deterministic'.

tasks.title: Support Hub-driven step-by-step execution mode
tasks.problem: Safest first production model is Hub-driven orchestration: Hub sends one authorized repair step at a time and evaluates result before sending next step.
implementation_notes: Implement before fully autonomous run_plan mutation mode if possible.
Each step result goes back to Hub for transition/approval/verification decision.
acceptance_criteria: Hub can create RepairStepExecutionEnvelope for a single plan step.
Worker runs exactly one step and returns RepairStepResult.
Hub records step result and decides next step based on state model.
Mutation step requires approval_ref in step envelope.
Worker cannot execute a future step out of order in Hub-driven mode.
test_requirements: E2E-ish test for two inspect steps and final classify/verify step.
anti_patterns: Do not give Worker the whole procedure and ask it to improvise the order in strict mode.
Do not skip Hub transition validation between steps.

tasks.title: Support bounded worker-run full procedure mode
tasks.problem: After step-by-step mode is safe, Worker may run full bounded procedure locally, but only with strict limits and approvals already encoded.
implementation_notes: Do not implement this before DRR-T014 unless clearly needed.
Full plan mode should remain disabled by default for mutation steps.
acceptance_criteria: Full procedure mode requires execution_mode='deterministic_repair_plan' and policy permission.
Runner enforces max_steps, max_runtime_seconds and max_mutation_steps.
Runner stops at approval_required step if approval_ref missing.
Runner stops after any failed required verification unless policy explicitly allows continue.
Full procedure mode can be disabled by config/feature flag.
anti_patterns: Do not allow unbounded repair loops.
Do not continue after failed verification.

tasks.title: Integrate RepairProcedureRunner with NativeWorkerRuntimeService
tasks.problem: Runner must be reachable through the real Worker runtime path, not only unit tests.
implementation_notes: Route execution_mode deterministic_repair_* to RepairProcedureRunner.
Non-repair modes must keep existing behavior.
acceptance_criteria: NativeWorkerRuntimeService detects deterministic repair execution mode and delegates to RepairProcedureRunner.
Repair runner output maps to WorkerResult/RepairExecutionResult and artifacts.
Missing repair_procedure in repair mode returns denied/degraded, not fallback to LLM command planning.
Existing command_plan/command_execute non-repair tests still pass.
Tests verify real service path invokes runner.
anti_patterns: Do not duplicate repair execution code inside NativeWorkerRuntimeService.
Do not fallback to LLM if repair runner rejects malformed plan.

tasks.title: Enforce repair safety classes in Hub and Worker
tasks.problem: Safety class exists in the model but must be enforced in both Hub plan generation and Worker execution.
implementation_notes: Enforce procedure safety_class and action_safety_class separately.
Use REPAIR_EXECUTION_SAFETY_POLICY.
acceptance_criteria: safe/inspect_only steps may run without approval if policy allows.
review_first and confirm_required steps require approval_ref.
high_risk steps require high-risk approval and stricter capability.
unknown action_safety_class is denied by default.
Worker rechecks safety class even if Hub created plan.
Tests cover safe, review_first, confirm_required, high_risk and unknown classes.
test_requirements: Safety class matrix tests Hub-side and Worker-side.
anti_patterns: Do not trust plan safety_class without Worker revalidation.
Do not allow unknown action class as safe.

tasks.title: Bind repair execution to approval scope
tasks.problem: Approval must be scoped to procedure_id, target_scope and session/job. A generic approval should not authorize unrelated repair actions.
implementation_notes: Use APPROVAL_REQUIREMENT_MODEL scope dimensions.
If approval service shape is different, create adapter/validator.
acceptance_criteria: ApprovalRef used by repair step includes procedure_id, target_scope and session/job/task scope.
Approval for one procedure cannot approve a different procedure_id.
Approval for one target path/service/port cannot approve a different target_scope.
Expired approval is rejected.
Approval decision appears in trace/audit.
anti_patterns: Do not use a single boolean approved flag for all repair mutations.
Do not allow Worker to mint approval refs.

tasks.title: Enforce unsafe action guardrails before every repair tool call
tasks.problem: UNSAFE_ACTION_GUARDRAIL_MODEL exists with blocked patterns, but it must be applied before commands/tools execute.
implementation_notes: Apply guardrails both when generating command templates and before tool execution.
Fail closed for unsafe/out-of-scope patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Commands matching blocked patterns like rm -rf /, mkfs, dd if=, shutdown, reboot are denied before execution.
Out-of-scope patterns like terraform/kubernetes/firewall/AD domain repair are escalated/review-required by default.
Guardrail denial includes reason_code and matched pattern id/category without leaking sensitive command args beyond safe snippet.
Unsafe command denial emits audit event.
Tests prove subprocess/tool executor is not called for blocked patterns.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on LLM to avoid dangerous commands.
Do not apply guardrails only Hub-side. Worker must recheck.

tasks.title: Add repair dry-run and proposal preview mode
tasks.problem: Operators need to preview deterministic repair steps, safety classes, approval needs and expected verification before execution.
implementation_notes: Dry-run must not call mutation tools.
It may validate tool availability and scope if safe.
acceptance_criteria: Dry-run returns RepairPreviewArtifact or RepairExecutionResult status='preview'.
Preview lists every step, mutation_candidate, safety_class, required_approval and verification probe.
Dry-run does not execute mutation steps.
Dry-run marks blocked/unknown/out-of-scope steps clearly.
Preview output is stable enough for UI display.
anti_patterns: Do not run low-risk mutations during preview.
Do not hide approval-required steps.

tasks.title: Make deterministic repair feature default-off or strict-gated
tasks.problem: Until E2E and safety enforcement are complete, deterministic repair execution should not be silently active for all users/tasks.
implementation_notes: Separate analyze-only from execute-capable flags.
Default may allow analysis/preview but not mutation execution.
acceptance_criteria: Config has flags for deterministic_repair_analysis_enabled, deterministic_repair_preview_enabled and deterministic_repair_execution_enabled or equivalent.
Execution-capable repair is disabled by default unless release decision says otherwise.
Disabled execution returns clear reason_code='deterministic_repair_execution_disabled'.
Analysis/preview can remain enabled if safe.
Diagnostics show feature flag states.
test_requirements: Feature flag tests for analysis enabled, preview enabled and execution disabled.
anti_patterns: Do not use one broad flag that turns on mutation execution accidentally.
Do not hide disabled state as generic failure.

tasks.title: Implement RepairVerificationRunner
tasks.problem: Verification is part of the deterministic model, but runtime needs explicit verifier that runs required probes after mutation and final verification before success.
implementation_notes: Use allowed_probes from REPAIR_VERIFICATION_MODEL.
Verification probes should also go through ToolInvocationEnvelope.
acceptance_criteria: VerificationRunner supports health_check, service_status, command_result and functional_probe types.
Unsupported probe type is denied/escalated, not skipped.
Mutation step requires post-step verification when verification_after_step is present.
Final verification is required before RepairExecutionResult status='success'.
Verification result includes evidence refs, pass/fail, reason_code and observed values.
test_requirements: Final verification missing prevents success test.
anti_patterns: Do not use command exit_code alone as final repair success for all procedures.
Do not skip verification when probe tool is unavailable.

tasks.title: Capture before/after evidence for mutation steps
tasks.problem: To prove repair success and support rollback/learning, mutation steps need before/after evidence snapshots where feasible.
implementation_notes: Before evidence is non-mutating probe before mutation.
After evidence is verification probe after mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Mutation step can declare before_probe and after_probe.
Runner records before_evidence_ref and after_evidence_ref when declared.
If before_probe fails, mutation does not proceed unless policy allows continue.
If after_probe fails, result is verification_failed or partial_success, not success.
Artifacts include before/after evidence references.
test_requirements: Before probe failure blocks mutation test.
anti_patterns: Do not mutate first and only then discover baseline was unknown.
Do not claim improvement without after evidence.

tasks.title: Add rollback hint and rollback proposal handling
tasks.problem: Repair procedures define rollback_hints but runtime needs to carry them into artifacts and optionally propose rollback when verification fails.
implementation_notes: Initial implementation should propose rollback, not automatically execute rollback unless explicit approval/policy exists.
acceptance_criteria: RepairExecutionResult includes rollback_hints for executed mutation steps.
Verification failure produces RollbackProposalArtifact or rollback_recommended field.
Automatic rollback execution is disabled by default.
Rollback execution, if later enabled, requires explicit repair.rollback capability and approval for mutation steps.
Tests prove failed verification creates rollback proposal but does not execute rollback by default.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-run rollback commands without approval.
Do not drop rollback hints from result artifacts.

tasks.title: Classify repair outcomes with standard labels
tasks.problem: Outcome learning needs consistent labels such as succeeded, partially_helped, failed, regressed. Runtime must derive these from verification and side effects.
implementation_notes: Use STANDARD_OUTCOME_LABELS.
Regressed should require evidence or explicit verification worsening indicator.
acceptance_criteria: Successful final verification maps to outcome_label='succeeded'.
Some step improvement but failed final verification maps to partially_helped or failed with reason.
Verification worse after mutation maps to regressed where evidence supports it.
Denied/approval_missing is not stored as failed repair unless execution actually attempted.
Outcome label and rationale appear in RepairExecutionResult.
anti_patterns: Do not label approval_required as failed repair outcome.
Do not label success without final verification.

tasks.title: Add RepairOutcomeDB and repository
tasks.problem: Outcome learning currently appears in-memory/target-model only. Repair outcomes must be persisted and queryable.
implementation_notes: If migrations are used, add migration. If SQLModel auto-create is used in tests, update accordingly.
Keep evidence refs instead of huge raw evidence blobs.
acceptance_criteria: RepairOutcomeDB includes id, created_at, updated_at, goal_id, task_id, worker_job_id, plan_id, procedure_id, signature_id, problem_class, platform_target, environment_facts_hash, execution_status, outcome_label, verification_evidence_refs, artifact_refs, trace_ref, regression_flag and metadata.
Repository supports save, get_by_id, query_by_problem_class, query_by_signature_id, query_by_procedure_id and recent_by_environment.
Outcome persistence works across service restart/test DB session, not only in-memory.
Indexes or query helpers support problem_class/procedure_id/signature_id lookup.
Tests cover save/query/update.
test_requirements: Query tests by problem_class, procedure_id, signature_id.
anti_patterns: Do not store repair outcome only in Python lists/dicts.
Do not persist huge raw logs directly in outcome row.

tasks.title: Implement RepairOutcomeService persistence path
tasks.problem: Runtime must call a service that persists outcome after repair execution and handles failures safely.
implementation_notes: Outcome persistence may happen Hub-side after WorkerResult returns, which is preferable for DB boundaries.
Worker should return enough structured data for Hub to persist outcome.
acceptance_criteria: RepairOutcomeService creates RepairOutcomeDB from RepairExecutionResult and plan metadata.
Persistence failure is surfaced as degraded outcome_persist_failed but does not falsely mark repair success as fully recorded.
RepairExecutionResult includes persisted_outcome_ref after successful persistence.
Outcome service redacts/sanitizes metadata before save.
Tests cover successful persist and DB failure path.
anti_patterns: Do not silently drop outcome persistence errors.
Do not let Worker write DB directly if Hub-owned persistence is the architectural boundary.

tasks.title: Build negative learning model from persisted outcomes
tasks.problem: build_negative_learning_model must query persisted RepairOutcomeDB instead of relying on in-memory session state.
implementation_notes: Negative learning should downgrade procedures that repeatedly fail/regress in similar environments.
Keep decision explainable.
acceptance_criteria: Negative learning model reads RepairOutcomeDB records by problem_class/procedure_id/signature_id/environment similarity.
Repeated failed or regressed outcomes lower procedure ranking or force review_required.
Succeeded outcomes can improve ranking but do not override safety approval requirements.
Model output includes evidence counts and reason codes.
Tests prove failed historical outcomes change ranking for similar environment.
test_requirements: Safety approval still required despite success history test.
anti_patterns: Do not let success history remove approval for high-risk procedures.
Do not use only current process memory for learning.

tasks.title: Add environment similarity query for repair outcomes
tasks.problem: Outcome memory model includes environment similarity weights, but persisted queries need implementation.
implementation_notes: Start with simple weighted score over platform_target, os_family, package_manager, service_state, container_state.
acceptance_criteria: RepairOutcomeService can score historical outcomes against current environment_facts.
Scores use ENVIRONMENT_SIMILARITY_MODEL weights or configured equivalent.
Missing fields reduce confidence but do not crash.
Query returns top similar outcomes with score and matched fields.
Tests cover exact match, partial match and missing fields.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every past failure as equally relevant to all environments.

tasks.title: Connect repair outcomes to ResultMemoryService safely
tasks.problem: Repair outcomes should become searchable memory, but only in redacted/scoped form with provenance and trust.
implementation_notes: RepairOutcomeDB remains source of truth; memory is retrieval projection.
acceptance_criteria: Successful persisted repair outcome can create redacted memory entry with source_type='repair_outcome'.
Failed/regressed outcomes can create negative-learning memory entry with clear label.
Memory entry references RepairOutcomeDB id and artifact refs.
Sensitive evidence is not copied raw into memory.
Retrieval can filter repair_outcome memories by problem_class/procedure_id.
test_requirements: Repair outcome to memory projection tests.
anti_patterns: Do not make memory the primary repair outcome database.
Do not copy raw logs/secrets into memory.

tasks.title: Add Repair artifacts to ArtifactTypeRegistry
tasks.problem: Deterministic repair needs typed artifacts for plan, preview, step result, verification, outcome and rollback proposal.
implementation_notes: Artifact schemas can reference repair contract models.
acceptance_criteria: Artifact types include RepairPlanArtifact, RepairPreviewArtifact, RepairStepResultArtifact, RepairVerificationArtifact, RepairOutcomeArtifact and RollbackProposalArtifact.
Each artifact type has required fields and schema validation.
RepairExecutionResult references these artifact ids/refs.
Artifact metadata includes plan_id, procedure_id, signature_id, safety_class, policy_decision_ref and context_hash.
Tests validate all repair artifact types.
test_requirements: Artifact type registry tests for repair artifacts.
anti_patterns: Do not store repair execution only as logs.
Do not use generic text artifact for all repair details.

tasks.title: Add Repair TraceBundle fields
tasks.problem: Repair execution needs traceability across signature matching, plan generation, approval, step execution, verification and outcome persistence.
implementation_notes: Use refs/hashes for evidence and context, not raw logs.
acceptance_criteria: TraceBundle includes repair_plan_id, procedure_id, signature_id, signature_confidence, safety_class, approval_refs, step_trace list, verification_trace, outcome_ref and escalation_reason where applicable.
Each step trace includes step_id, step_type, tool_invocation_hash/ref, started_at, ended_at, status and reason_code.
Trace exists for success, denial, approval_required, verification_failed, escalated and timeout.
Trace excludes raw secrets and unbounded evidence text.
Tests reconstruct repair flow from trace.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on scattered logs to understand repair execution.
Do not store unredacted command output in trace.

tasks.title: Emit audit events for deterministic repair lifecycle
tasks.problem: Repair actions can mutate systems and therefore need audit at every sensitive lifecycle point.
implementation_notes: Use stable event names and correlation ids.
Audit failures for mutation steps should fail closed according to policy.
acceptance_criteria: Audit events include repair_signature_matched, repair_plan_generated, repair_approval_required, repair_step_started, repair_step_completed, repair_step_denied, repair_verification_completed, repair_outcome_persisted, repair_escalated and repair_rollback_proposed.
Mutation step audit event is emitted before tool execution and after result.
Events include goal_id, task_id, plan_id, procedure_id, step_id, safety_class, policy_decision_ref and approval_ref where relevant.
Events exclude secrets/raw evidence.
Tests verify audit events for allowed, denied and verification_failed flows.
test_requirements: Audit event tests for repair lifecycle.
anti_patterns: Do not audit only successful repairs.
Do not execute mutation when pre-mutation audit fails.

tasks.title: Add repair diagnostics/read model
tasks.problem: Operators need to see repair engine readiness: signatures, catalog, feature flags, outcome persistence, runner availability and safety policy state.
implementation_notes: No secrets/raw logs.
acceptance_criteria: Diagnostics include deterministic_repair_analysis_enabled, preview_enabled, execution_enabled, signature_count, playbook_count, procedure_count, outcome_persistence_ready, runner_ready and last_error_code.
Diagnostics include safety policy summary and approval-required classes.
Diagnostics identify missing repository/table for RepairOutcomeDB as degraded.
Diagnostics are consumable by UI/TUI/status command.
Tests verify diagnostics fields and redaction.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw repair logs/evidence in diagnostics.
Do not hide missing persistence.

tasks.title: Add API endpoints for repair analysis, preview and execution
tasks.problem: Deterministic repair needs explicit API surfaces instead of being hidden in goal context.
implementation_notes: Endpoints may be nested under tasks/goals if project style prefers.
Keep execute endpoint approval-gated.
acceptance_criteria: API supports analyze repair evidence -> signature/outcome response.
API supports create repair preview -> RepairPreviewArtifact/RepairPlanArtifact.
API supports execute approved repair plan or step -> Worker job/result.
API supports fetch repair outcome/history for task/goal/procedure.
Execute endpoint requires admin/operator permission or configured repair permission.
Malformed input returns structured error, not stack trace.
test_requirements: API tests for analyze, preview, execute denied without approval, fetch outcome.
anti_patterns: Do not trigger mutation execution from analyze endpoint.
Do not expose execute repair endpoint without auth/approval.

tasks.title: Expose BlueprintRoleDB.config safely in API/UI or advanced editor
tasks.problem: BlueprintRoleDB.config contains preferred_backend, execution_mode, capability_defaults, risk_profile and verification_defaults, but UI may not expose it. This makes role behavior API/DB-only and hard to operate.
implementation_notes: If Angular path differs, locate blueprint role editor/list components.
Use advanced JSON editor only if validated schema exists. Prefer structured fields.
acceptance_criteria: Blueprint role API create/update accepts and validates config fields preferred_backend, execution_mode, capability_defaults, risk_profile, verification_defaults.
UI displays role config in advanced section or structured controls.
Invalid capability_defaults/risk_profile/execution_mode are rejected with clear validation error.
Seed/default role config remains preserved when edited through UI.
Tests cover API roundtrip for BlueprintRoleDB.config.
test_requirements: Backend API tests for role config create/update.
Frontend unit/snapshot test if frontend tests exist.
anti_patterns: Do not expose unvalidated arbitrary JSON for security-sensitive execution config in normal UI.
Do not silently drop config fields on UI save.

tasks.title: Expose agent capabilities, worker_roles and execution_limits safely
tasks.problem: Agent capabilities, worker_roles, execution_limits, registration_validated and validation_errors may be API/DB-only or not editable/displayed in UI, making worker selection and validation opaque.
implementation_notes: Show validation fields read-only unless admin repair/revalidation flow exists.
Capabilities/roles should be validated against registry.
acceptance_criteria: Agent API create/update supports capabilities, worker_roles and execution_limits with validation.
UI can edit capabilities and worker_roles through constrained multi-select or advanced validated editor.
UI can edit execution_limits such as max_parallel_tasks with sane min/max.
registration_validated and validation_errors are displayed read-only in UI.
Invalid role/capability/limit returns structured validation error and is not persisted.
Tests cover API roundtrip and invalid values.
anti_patterns: Do not let UI silently omit advanced fields and overwrite them with null/defaults.
Do not make validation_errors editable by normal users.

tasks.title: Add operator view for deterministic repair path
tasks.problem: Operator needs visibility into detected signature, selected playbook/procedure, approval requirements, preview, step status, verification and outcome history.
implementation_notes: Can start backend-only if frontend path is not ready.
UI should clearly mark analysis, preview, approval and execution states.
acceptance_criteria: Operator view/API shows session_summary, path_visibility, proposal_preview and history_inspection as defined by OPERATOR_VIEW_MODEL.
View shows signature id/confidence/problem_class and why it matched.
View shows procedure steps with safety class and approval requirement.
View shows verification result and persisted outcome label.
View clearly distinguishes deterministic path from LLM escalation/recommendation.
test_requirements: API read-model test for operator repair view.
anti_patterns: Do not hide repair safety class from operator.
Do not present LLM suggestion as deterministic procedure.

tasks.title: Add repair config validation to bundle import/export
tasks.problem: Blueprint/team bundle import/export should preserve and validate repair-related role config, otherwise deterministic repair teams can drift when exported/imported.
implementation_notes: Ensure role.config survives export/import and is validated.
acceptance_criteria: Bundle export includes BlueprintRole config fields relevant to repair execution.
Bundle import validates repair role config and rejects invalid capability/risk/execution_mode values.
Dry-run import reports config diffs.
Roundtrip export/import preserves repair role config.
Tests cover roundtrip and invalid config.
anti_patterns: Do not strip advanced role config during bundle import/export.

tasks.title: Add full E2E deterministic repair flow test
tasks.problem: The critical missing proof is end-to-end: Goal -> Plan -> Tasks -> Worker -> Execution -> Verification -> Outcome persistence.
implementation_notes: Use fake safe environment and mocked tool executor; no real system mutation.
Port conflict or service_start_failure inspect-only/low-risk procedure is a good first scenario.
acceptance_criteria: Test starts from goal/task containing deterministic repair evidence.
Hub matches signature and selects problem_class deterministically.
Hub creates RepairProcedureExecutionPlan.
Worker receives ExecutionEnvelope with repair_procedure, not plain context only.
Worker executes inspect/probe step(s) via ToolInvocationEnvelope.
VerificationRunner runs final verification.
RepairOutcomeDB row is persisted.
WorkerResult/RepairExecutionResult contains typed artifacts and trace refs.
No LLM is used for high-confidence deterministic path.
anti_patterns: Do not test only individual services and call it E2E.
Do not allow E2E to pass if worker receives repair plan only as unstructured text.

tasks.title: Add E2E approval-required repair test
tasks.problem: Review-first/high-risk repair must stop for approval and only continue with correctly scoped approval.
implementation_notes: Use mocked mutation tool that would record if called.
acceptance_criteria: Without approval_ref, review_first mutation step returns needs_approval and tool is not called.
With wrong procedure_id approval, step is denied and tool is not called.
With correct scoped approval, step can run if all other policy checks pass.
Audit records approval_required and approval_consumed events.
Outcome is not persisted as failed repair merely because approval was missing.
anti_patterns: Do not let generic approval authorize all repair steps.
Do not run mutation before approval validation.

tasks.title: Add E2E verification failure and negative learning test
tasks.problem: Failed/regressed repair must persist outcome and influence future ranking or review requirements.
implementation_notes: Use fake verification failure after low-risk mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Repair mutation executes under approval/policy but final verification fails.
RepairExecutionResult status is verification_failed or failed, not success.
RepairOutcomeDB persists outcome_label='failed' or 'regressed' as appropriate.
Next plan generation for similar environment uses persisted outcome to lower procedure ranking or mark review_required.
Trace and artifacts show failed verification evidence.
anti_patterns: Do not discard failed repair outcomes.
Do not claim partial output as success when verification fails.

tasks.title: Add malformed input and failure-mode E2E tests
tasks.problem: Repair runtime must handle bad evidence, missing environment, unavailable tools, audit failure and persistence failure safely.
implementation_notes: Use fakes/mocks.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed evidence returns invalid_evidence_payload and no Worker execution.
Unknown problem class escalates/review-required without mutation.
Required tool unavailable returns degraded/tool_unavailable and no mutation.
Audit failure before mutation blocks execution.
Outcome persistence failure is surfaced as degraded and does not pretend learning occurred.
anti_patterns: Do not use broad try/except that hides root cause.
Do not continue repair after audit/tool/persistence critical failure.

tasks.title: Add deterministic repair security regression suite
tasks.problem: Repair is high-risk. A focused regression suite must prove no common bypass is possible.
implementation_notes: No live system mutation; monkeypatch executors to detect calls.
acceptance_criteria: Suite proves Worker cannot execute repair from unstructured context text alone.
Suite proves tampered plan hash is denied.
Suite proves unsafe command patterns are denied before execution.
Suite proves high-risk step without approval does not call tool executor.
Suite proves unknown step_type is denied.
Suite proves LLM escalation cannot directly execute raw commands.
Suite runs without external services.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on happy-path E2E only.
Do not let security suite require real sudo/system commands.

tasks.title: Write deterministic repair runtime docs and rollout checklist
tasks.problem: Docs must clearly distinguish current deterministic analysis, preview, step execution, full runner mode, approval, verification and learning status.
implementation_notes: Use implemented/partial/planned sections.
Include diagrams for Hub-driven and Worker-run modes.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain why unstructured context is not sufficient for deterministic repair.
Docs show Hub-driven step-by-step flow and Worker RepairProcedureRunner flow.
Docs list feature flags and default-off/default-preview behavior.
Docs list approval and safety class rules.
Docs list E2E/security test commands.
Rollout checklist requires DRR-T040 through DRR-T044 before enabling mutation execution.
test_requirements: Docs command snippets reviewed against real test names.
anti_patterns: Do not document planned Worker-run full procedure mode as production-ready before tests pass.
Do not hide that mutation execution is feature-gated.

definition_of_done_global: Hub creates RepairProcedureExecutionPlan as structured data from deterministic analysis.
ExecutionEnvelope carries repair_procedure payload for deterministic repair modes.
Worker RepairProcedureRunner exists and refuses unstructured repair-only context as execution authority.
Every repair step goes through ToolInvocationEnvelope and WorkerPreflightGate/ToolRegistry.
Safety classes and approval scopes are enforced by Hub and Worker.
Mutation steps require verification and cannot report success without final verification.
RepairOutcomeDB persists outcomes across process restarts.
Negative learning uses persisted outcomes, not in-memory lists.
Repair artifacts, trace and audit cover the full lifecycle.
API exposes analyze, preview, execute and outcome/history surfaces safely.
UI/API no longer silently hides critical role/agent execution config where operators need it.
E2E tests prove high-confidence deterministic repair works without LLM and without unstructured-context execution authority.
Security regression suite proves approval, unsafe command, tampered plan, unknown step and LLM-command bypasses are blocked.
summary_notes: This track directly addresses the gap that deterministic repair currently behaves like structured Hub recommendation unless the Worker receives and executes machine-readable repair procedures.

The safest first production model is Hub-driven step-by-step execution. Worker-run full procedure mode should remain feature-gated until step-by-step execution, verification, approval and outcome persistence are proven.
Repair mutation execution should not be enabled until the minimum_safe_slice and security regression suite are complete.
